I'll start by warming up and getting familiar with the current state of the repository.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && git fetch origin main 2>/dev/null; git merge origin/main --no-edit 2>/dev/null; echo "Git sync complete"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user said "Warmup" - this is a request to get oriented with the codebase following the session protocol. Let me follow the start protocol:

1. git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
2. Read env for today's date (already have it: 2025-11-27)
3. git log -3
4. ls tasks/*.md and resume the newest active task
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && git log -3 --oneline</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && ls tasks/*.md 2>/dev/null || echo "No task files found"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

Let me check the tasks directory structure:

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && dir tasks /B 2>nul</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\tasks\**\*.md</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\tasks\master-implementation-status.md</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
